channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Earth's first animals evolved slowly until sexual reproduction changed everything
item.description: Earth's earliest animals may have stalled evolution through asexual reproduction, creating stable communities with little change.

item.title: Popular joint supplement glucosamine linked to faster Alzheimer's progression
item.description: A major study suggests glucosamine, a common joint pain supplement, may accelerate the progression from mild cognitive impairment to Alzheimer's.

item.title: Scientists propose a radical new theory for the origin of life on Earth
item.description: Researchers suggest tiny mineral nanoparticles may have driven early Earth chemistry toward the first building blocks of life.

item.title: Scientists mapped every neural connection in a fruit fly and found a surprise
item.description: A new connectome maps all neural connections in an adult fruit fly's central nervous system, offering an unprecedented view of its structure.
